name: Sejong (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity and a bright future for the new administrative capital
the rivers and sky surrounding Sejong, expressing openness and tranquillity
harmony with nature and the surrounding fields and mountains
warmth, vitality, and the rising sun

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised multicolour emblem composed of an abstract rendering of King Sejong's name and form, with green, blue and orange wave-like elements suggesting people in motion.
history: Sejong Special Self-Governing City (Sejong Teukbyeol-Jachisi), named after King Sejong the Great (1397–1450), was established on 1 July 2012 as the new administrative capital of South Korea, hosting many central-government ministries. The city flag and emblem were adopted at the same time. The motif stylises Hangul letters from the city's name and represents harmony among nature, citizens and government.

features.symbolism: stylised Hangul letters from 세종 (Sejong) combined with green, blue and orange wave forms representing people, rivers and sun; together they express harmony between nature, citizens and the new administrative capital
